key_reasoning: Base rate is effectively zero — no such agreement (formal bilateral, oil-for-yuan with explicit volume commitment, publicly announced by both parties) has been signed in 20+ years of significant Iran-China oil trade; actual mechanisms are informal intermediaries and deliberate opacity. Current conditions push the estimate below even the prior: (1) US Treasury sanctioned Chang Guang Satellite Technology and associated entities on May 8-10 for enabling IRGC targeting, creating maximum political cost for Beijing to formalise Iran ties in the same window; (2) Trump-Xi summit in Beijing on May 14-15 — four days from now — means China will not issue a provocation that directly undercuts the summit's agenda of US pressure on Iran to reopen Hormuz; (3) the Araghchi-Wang Yi meeting on 6 May was diplomatic positioning, not economic deal-making, and China is explicitly calling for 'comprehensive ceasefire', a mediator posture incompatible with formal economic integration signalling. Fermi decomposition: P(China political will now) ~0.04 × P(Iran political will) ~0.35 × P(deal in 30 days) ~0.20 × P(public by both) ~0.25 = 0.0007. Bayesian chain from 2% prior, applying LR for fresh US sanctions (0.30), summit imminence (0.20), active Iran-US diplomacy via Pakistan (0.50) yields posterior ~0.06%. The 3% point estimate is a disciplined upward adjustment for Chinese decision-making uncertainty and the possibility of a confrontational summit outcome triggering countermoves.

framework_specific.outside_view: Zero historical instances of publicly announced formal Iran-China oil-for-yuan settlement with volume commitments in 20+ years of significant trade; base rate 0/240 months = 0%, adjusted to 1-2% prior to allow for unprecedented conflict conditions
framework_specific.bayesian_chain: Prior 2% → LR(US sanctions Chinese firms for Iran, same cycle) 0.30 → LR(Trump-Xi summit 4 days away) 0.20 → LR(active Iran-US diplomatic exchange via Pakistan) 0.50 → posterior ~0.06%; point estimate rounded up to 3% for genuine model uncertainty
framework_specific.fermi_decomposition: P(China political will) × P(Iran political will) × P(agreement in 30 days) × P(public by both) = 0.04 × 0.35 × 0.20 × 0.25 = 0.07%
framework_specific.specification_constraint: Scenario explicitly excludes crude swaps and informal credit — the actual mechanisms China uses. Formal + public + both parties + explicit volume commitment is the maximum observable commitment, precisely what China has strategically avoided even with closer partners
framework_specific.anti_bias_flags: Guarded against availability bias from general China-Iran cooperation narrative; applied scope discipline — the scenario's four-component requirement (formal AND yuan AND volume commitment AND both parties public) requires all sub-conditions to hold simultaneously; Trump-Xi summit is a strong near-term binding constraint not to be dismissed
framework_specific.what_would_change_mind: Post-summit diplomatic collapse triggering Beijing-Washington confrontation; or evidence of an existing draft agreement text circulating in Chinese state media (would push estimate above 15%)
